dishes.name: Vegetarian - Eggplant Parmesan

dishes.notes:
Ingredients

  • 1 tbsp butter
  • 1/2 cup water
  • 1 tbsp honey 
  • 1 tbsp brown sugar
  • Shrimp 
  • 2 eggs
  • 1/2 cup corn starch
  • 3 tbsp mayo 
  • 1 tbsp honey
  • 1 tsp lemon juice

 

Instructions

  • Caramelize butter with water, honey, and sugar over medium heat (wait for it to bubble)
  • Add walnuts and cook for 5 minutes
  • Remove and separate on pan

 

  • Season shrimp with salt and pepper
  • Scramble eggs and coat shrimp
  • Coat shrimp with corn starch
  • Pan fry shrimp and return walnuts to pan
  • Coat with sauce

dishes.name: Chicken - Ritzy Baked Chicken

dishes.notes:
Ingredients:

  • 2½  (325 grams) cups all-purpose flour, plus more to dust
  • 1 tsp salt
  • ½ tbsp sugar
  • 1 cup of very cold unsalted butter (2 sticks), cut into ½ inch 
  • 4 to 8 tbsp ice water 

Instructions:

  • Pulse dry ingredients to combine
  • Add butter and pulse until coarse crumbs form with some lima bean size pieces; should remain dry and powdery
  • Slowly add ice water, 1 tbsp at a time, and pulse until dough comes together; should be moist, but not wet
    • If you pinch some of the dough and it holds together, it’s ready.
  • Gather dough together into a ball (do not knead); divide in half and flatten to disks
  • Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 1 hour
  • Rolling out:
    • Remove dough from refrigerator and let sit for 5 minutes
    • Flour surface, dough, and rolling pin
    • Roll to fit pie dish with 1 inch edge over the circumference 
    • Transfer to pie dish and trim if necessary
    • Tuck the edge inwards, but don’t crimp until the top is on

dishes.description: Made in the skillet with brown butter

dishes.notes:
Ingredients

  • onion
  • garlic
  • thyme 
  • CRP
  • diced tomatoes 
  • crushed tomatoes
  • stock
  • red wine
  • fish (already cooked)
  • salt and pepper
  • shrimp & scallop
  • lemon
  • parsley 
  • basil

 

Instructions:

  • cut onions, garlic, basil, parsely, thyme 
  • pan fry salmon (salt & pepper)
  • sautee onions, add garlic, then thyme, crp, 
  • add tomatoes
  • add stock and red wine
  • simmer for 45 mins
  • add fish and then season with S&P
  • add shrimp and scallop
  • lemon juice
  • add parsley and basil

dishes.description: Best cookies ever
dishes.name: Claire Saffitz's Chocolate Chip Cookies
dishes.notes:
Ingredients:

2 sticks unsalted butter (8 oz / 227g), cut into tablespoons
2 tablespoons heavy cream, half-and-half, or whole milk (1 oz / 28g)
2 cups all-purpose flour (9.2 oz / 260g)
2 teaspoons Diamond Crystal kosher salt (0.22 oz / 6g)
1 teaspoon baking soda (0.21 oz / 6g)
3/4 cup packed dark brown sugar (5.3 oz / 150g)
3/4 cup granulated sugar (5.3 oz / 150g)
2 large eggs (3.5 oz / 100g), cold from the refrigerator 
1 tablespoon vanilla extract
5 ounces (142g) bittersweet chocolate disks, half coarsely chopped
5 ounces (142g) milk chocolate disks, half coarsely chopped

Instructions:

  • brown butter over low heat; pour into mixing bowl
  • add 2 tbsp of heavy cream or milk and let it cool down
  • add equal amounts of white and brown sugar; mix
  • add eggs one at a time and mix
  • add vanilla and mix
  • in a separate bowl:  mix flour, baking soda, salt (if morton use only 1 tsp!) 
  • add dry ingredients into mixing bowl and mix
  • add chocolate and stir in 
  • portion out dough and chill for at least a couple hours in the fridge
  • pre-heat oven to 350 degrees
  • bake for 10 minutes then switch racks to ensure even cooking
  • bake for 18-22 minutes total until golden brown edge

dishes.notes:
- 3/4 cup oat milk
- 1 cup nuts (I've been doing half almonds, half cashews but I think it would be fine to use all cashews). Toast them in a skillet for a few minutes first
- 1 medium red bell pepper, core removed
- 1 chipotle pepper + a bit of the sauce (I recommend buying a can of chipotles in adobo sauce from the Mexican aisle. You can save what you don't use in a tupperware)
- 1/4 cup nutritional yeast
- Salt to taste (up to a teaspoon)
- 1/2 teaspoon of turmeric (optional)
 
Blend all ingredients 5/6 minutes, until the mixture is physically hot. Lasts about a week in the fridge. Tasty cold or hot!
